title: Music Department Annual Update - 2021-2022 SY (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Music Department Annual Update
Prepared by – Ron Slabbinck, Ed.D., Music Faculty
Reviewed/Edited by – Valerie Roberts, Dean, LAS
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
A. Most significant challenge – Continued Implications of COVID-19
- Reduction in class offerings (ensembles) and reduction in student enrollment
- Loss of live, in person instruction, rehearsal, and performance
- Uncertainty surrounding student/community course interest
- Safety returning in person, protocols, masks, music making as a risk of disease spread
B. Most significant growth/learning – COVID-19
- Developed non-traditional means for music instruction (traditionally face-to-face
adapted to remote)
- More courses now able to be taught remotely
- New pedagogy and instruction techniques discovered in remote instruction can be
applied to traditional face-to-face instruction
- Creatively integrate online and in person (hybrid)
- Leverage Technology for music instruction
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
- Transition from Curricunet to eLumen in 2018 resulted in an auto-update of all
courses in Fall 2018. However, actual updated did not occur and therefore MUS
courses will be updated and made current early in 2021. This Curricunet to eLumen
transfer has also revealed other issues, as some SLOs did not transfer correctly, and
some did not transfer at all. Therefore, as Courses are updated, SLOs will be
examined against archived CORs to ensure that all SLOs are present. Then, any SLOs
that are outdated or need updating can be addressed.
- The degree course sequence/requirement modifications indicated in last year’s
comprehensive Program Review will be submitted to the Curriculum Committee
early in 2021. COVID-19 paused this action, as focus and attention was necessarily
directed at modifying instruction to be delivered remotely. Once complete, the new
degree course sequence will ensure students a path to graduation with either a Music
Degree for Transfer, or a local AA in Music in not more than two full academic
school years.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Obstacles

-- 1 of 4 --

title: Music Department Annual Update - 2021-2022 SY (Part 2)
text:
- COVID-19
o Listed Above
- Shrinking Local H.S. music programs
o COVID
o budget constraints
o diminishing student population
o elementary and high school programs closing
o impending K-12 Music Faculty retirements
- Decreased College Budget
o Multiple factors contribute to this issue
- Uncertainty surrounding budget
o How many classes being offered?
o Will travel be reinstated?
o Will ensembles be at/near full capacity?
o What is behind in being replaced/repaired/maintained?
Support
- Dean and Vice President of Academic Affairs
o Understand value of music and the arts
o Supportive of creative scheduling/course offerings
- Theater Construction Project
o Students feel valued and safe
o Facility will need to be brought up to code and made safe
- Additional Adjunct Faculty member
o Has yielded more flexibility in course offerings and times
o Functions as both Adjunct Instructor and EIA for ensembles and music majors
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Submit a CQIP if you are requesting a
budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly budget allocations).
The following budget items are carried over from previous Annual updates and Program
Reviews. CQIPs will be submitted to resolve all of the line items that had been zeroed out.
• 2126 Class-Overtime
o For all music performances (on and off campus), Tech and stage support requires
additional staff
o Twelve Concerts/year
o Six Dress Rehearsals/year
o Growth - Add 2-4 additional Concerts
o This did not happen due to continued uncertainty surrounding COVID, however,
we hope to grow back to this level.
• 2321 Student Help (Noninstr) - District

-- 2 of 4 --

o Create inventory of music and instruments
o Assist with clerical/photocopying
o Assist with set-up for concerts and performances
o Counselors for Summer Jazz/Showchoir Camp
o Help organize, categorize, and purge music department materials
